ddls:
CREATE TABLE "compressors" (
  "compressor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compressor */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the compressor (e.g., CSD 90 T SFC, CSD 110 T SFC) */,
  "working_pressure" REAL /* Working pressure of the compressor in bar */,
  "flow_rate" TEXT /* Flow rate of the compressor at working pressure in m³/min */,
  "max_positive_pressure" REAL /* Maximum positive pressure of the compressor in bar */,
  "nominal_motor_output" INTEGER /* Nominal motor output of the compressor in kW */,
  "refrigeration_dryer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the refrigeration dryer used in the compressor */,
  "compressed_air_connection_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressed air connection used in the compressor */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the compressor record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the compressor record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compressor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compressor_specs" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compressor specification */,
  "compressor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressor the specification belongs to */,
  "spec_name" TEXT /* Name of the specification (e.g., Dimensions, Compressed air connection) */,
  "spec_value" TEXT /* Value of the specification */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of the specification value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compressor_specs_compressor_id FOREIGN KEY ("compressor_id") REFERENCES compressors ("compressor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compressor_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compressor type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the compressor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compressor type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "refrigeration_dryers" (
  "dryer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each refrigeration dryer */,
  "dryer_name" TEXT /* Name of the refrigeration dryer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the refrigeration dryer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dryer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compressed_air_connections" (
  "connection_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compressed air connection */,
  "connection_name" TEXT /* Name of the compressed air connection */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compressed air connection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("connection_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compressor_instances" (
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compressor instance */,
  "compressor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressor model */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* ID of the site where the compressor is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the compressor was installed */,
  "commissioning_date" TEXT /* Date the compressor was commissioned */,
  "decommissioning_date" TEXT /* Date the compressor was decommissioned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compressor_instances_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compressor_instances_compressor_id FOREIGN KEY ("compressor_id") REFERENCES compressors ("compressor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance data entry */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressor instance */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the performance data was recorded */,
  "flow_rate" REAL /* Flow rate of the compressor in m³/min */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Pressure of the compressor in bar */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Energy consumption of the compressor in kW */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature of the compressor in °C */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity of the compressor in % */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_data_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES compressor_instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance record */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressor instance */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance was performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance performed (e.g., routine, repair) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance in currency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES compressor_instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, maintenance personnel, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the performance data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES performance_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_schedules" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance schedule */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* ID of the compressor instance */,
  "schedule_date" TEXT /* Date the maintenance is scheduled */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance scheduled (e.g., routine, repair) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance scheduled */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance schedule (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_schedules_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES compressor_instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* ID of the maintenance schedule */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., reminder, alert) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_schedule_id FOREIGN KEY ("schedule_id") REFERENCES maintenance_schedules ("schedule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table affected by the change */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record affected by the change */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., insert, update, delete) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)
